criteria.label: Explain what a random seed does and name two ML steps it affects.
criteria.example: Write: 'Seed 42 fixes PRNG output so shuffles repeat.' Name train_test_split random_state and torch weight initialization.

criteria.label: Write a minimal multi-library seed block for a PyTorch + sklearn script.
criteria.example: Provide 5 lines: random.seed(0), np.random.seed(0), torch.manual_seed(0), torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(0), comment to call before load_data().

criteria.label: List four metadata fields to log for reproducibility besides metrics.
criteria.example: Seed(s), git SHA, requirements lock or Docker tag, Python version — optionally CUDA driver version.

criteria.label: Contrast why a single seed is enough for dev sweeps but multi-seed runs help final benchmarks.
criteria.example: One paragraph: fixed seed isolates lr during tuning; 5 seeds report mean ± std when stochastic training variance matters for claims.
